rulings.Case Title: CHARLES GILPIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant The Goodyear Tire Rubber Company'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, March 15, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1, Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Co.¿s person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and request for sanctions is continued to March 22 per agreement of the parties. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES DENHAM et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay And Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Notice Of Robert Cantley And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, March 15, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2, Defendant Golden Gate Drywall¿s motion to stay and quash plaintiff¿s deposition notice of Robert Cantley is granted. Plaintiffs did not show good cause under C.C.P. § 2025.610 to depose Mr. Cantley a second time. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LIMA VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION To Disqualify Berry & Berry As Coordinating Defense Counsel, On In The Alternative, Motion For An Order Limiting Berry & Berry To Activities Authorized By General Order 41 And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, March 15, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3, Plaintiffs¿ amended motion to disqualify Berry & Berry as Coordinating Defense Counsel and alternate motion to limit Berry & Berry to activities authorized by General Order 41 are denied. Plaintiffs have not made a showing that General Order 41, statutes, or court rules prohibit Berry & Berry from bringing discovery motions or joining a specific defendant as associated counsel. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LIMA VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Designated Defense Counsels Second Motion For Orders:Striking Plaintiffs Unlawful Objections To Subpoenas Of His Medical And Employment Records; Compelling Plaintiff'S Counsel To Write To Records Custodian For Each Subpoenaed Location Instructing Custodian To Disregard Plaintiff'S Objection And To Comply With Designated Defense Counsel'S Subpoena; Imposing Sanctions Against Plaintiff'S C
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, March 15, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3, Berry & Berry¿s motion to strike Plaintiff¿s objections to subpoenas and compel Plaintiff¿s Counsel to write to records custodian is denied as the underlying issue is moot. Berry & Berry¿s request for sanctions against Keller, Fishback and Jackson is partially granted for the amount of $4,550 pursuant to CCP § 2023.010 and CCP § 2023.030(a). Plaintiff¿s counsel failed to meet and confer as required and improperly used discovery tactics. Berry & Berry¿s request for a protective order is granted. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.= (503/TLJ)
